sourceRefs.fallbackText: Keys in key/value pairs of JSON are always of the type str . When a dictionary is converted into JSON, all the keys of the dictionary are coerced to strings. As a result of this, if a dictionary is converted into JSON and then back into a dictionary, the dictionary may not equal the original one. That is, loads(dumps(x)) != x if x has non-string keys.

If ensure_ascii is false, all characters will be output as-is, except for the characters that must be escaped: quotation mark, reverse solidus, and the control characters U+0000 through U+001F.
